entry.title: Variance delta symbol
entry.content: Hi, I have created a KPI below to show the number of compliments for a particular period. Underneath, I have a variance comparing it to the previous period. Is there a way I can change the delta symbol to something else? Many thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP S4 HANA Vs EC Payroll
entry.content:
Hello,

The "best" approach depends on the various conditions. The comment that I am giving based on my own experiences. At the end, I strongly believe that you should be considering this with your project team, SAP representatives, and/or SAP service provider.

Some items to think about regarding your infrastructure:

  1. Are you planning on separating your HR system from the rest?
  2. Do you have any business requirements for non-HR modules, which would require HR specific data/calculations etc.?
  3. Do you have any resources for working on integrations?

ECP is a cloud solution, which has a dynamic integration with SuccessFactors, and the integration effort between these two systems are kind of minimal compared to the integration between on-prem and SuccessFactors. At the end, the approaches are similar; but some technical details are different, and of course these differences reflects to the efforts you will be spending.

There is no right or wrong answer at this point. Pros and cons change depending on the answers for the questions above; and of course on the recourses you can allocate. If you are eventually going to separate your HR system, then having a standalone ECP environment would be safer, and your integration effort for HR module will be less (for the PTP part where you integrate ECP and SF). However, you would also need to consider integrating the ECP environment with your ERP system as well depending on which HR data is required for other modules. On the other hand, if you won't separate your environments and want to continue with on-prem, in this case HR module specific integration would require a lot more integration effort; however the integration with the other modules in this case would not be a problem.

Hope that at least this gives you the general idea.

Altug

entry.title: 3rd party application integration with SAP Service Cloud V2
entry.content:
Hi Experts,
We are trying to integrate a third-party application to SAP Service Cloud V2. Our requirement is to Integrate the application and embed its screen. We require this embedded screen to be always accessible similar to productivity tools (CTI Widget, Built-In Support, Notifications etc) that are always available irrespective of which screen we are in.

We tried different routes like Mashup, separate tab etc but those are not feasible as those will be available only when we will we select them. Our requirement is to keep the embedded screen always available. Any pointers with regards to this will be really appreciated.

Best Regards,

Imran

entry.title: Re: Overflow when converting from "1.34257e+11" IN MR8M R...
entry.content:
Hello,

During posting, the values are calculated, resulting in very large decimal places.

In the screenshot, the number 1.34257e+11 (equivalent to 134,257,000,000) can be seen. This is the notation of scientific or exponential notation. The error “Conversion Overflow” means that the number is too large for the SAP data element. If the calculated numbers are correct, then it must be fixed in the standard.

I also found the following note:  2065313

entry.title: Re: Unable side menu on Zebra - RF - Personas
entry.content:
Hi Tamas,

Thanks for the quick feedback

I have added this code as per your suggestion on the onLoad event and I am getting the following message: Property 'hide' does not exist on type "IGuiComponent"'.

I get this error message A LOT when I work with RF tcodes.

How do I fix it?
Thanks

entry.title: Re: Overflow when converting from "1.34257e+11" IN MR8M R...
entry.content: Thanks Tukaj.  I'll Check

entry.title: MRP for Jobworker in SAP B1
entry.content:
Hello,
I want to run an MRP for Jobwork where I am giving raw material to the Jobworker and receive Finished Goods. I am trying to run a scenario where  I am getting recommendations only for the purchase order of the Finished Goods from Jobworker but I also need to procure the Raw material required to make this Finished Good and make semi finished goods made inhouse that we send to the jobworker
Please help.
Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Z1 ledger and accounting principle related issue while posting Depreciation
entry.content: Did you solve your issue?

entry.title: Re: Hana Cloud Dashboard "Uh oh. Something went amiss."
entry.content: I figured out the issue, need to add entitlements for Hana tools and then create hana cloud instance with plan as "tools". After that you switch to the new UI

entry.title: Re: Issue with concatenate. No blanks at the end. RESPECTING BLANKS not working fully.
entry.content:
I don't think LS_TAB is of type STRING. Use CONCATENATE RESPECTING BLANKS to fill the STRING-type variable.

If trailing blanks of a STRING-type variable are not transferred, it would be a bug because TRANSFER - ABAP Keyword Documentation (sap.com) says, and so you would have to contact the SAP Support.

If the file was opened as a text file or a legacy text file, the trailing blank characters are deleted for all data objects, except for those of data type string.

entry.title: SAP PaPM Cloud Universal Model: What’s New as of 2024-09-02?
entry.content:
Hello Community Members! 

Discover what’s new in SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud – Universal Model (SAP PaPM Cloud UM)! This post highlights the latest features and enhancements now available for you to explore. 

Let's dive in! 

Model 

Model Entity: 

Enhanced Model Entity Restrictions for Improved Data Management  

We’re thrilled to announce a significant update to the Model Entity functionality, introducing a new restriction feature designed to refine data management capabilities. The newly added "Update and Read" restriction allows users to modify existing records while preventing the creation or deletion of records. This enhancement is ideal for scenarios where users need to adjust current data but should not introduce new entries or remove existing ones. 

With this restriction, users can make updates to existing records and view data but cannot create new records or delete existing ones. The UI will reflect this by removing both the Create and Delete buttons when the restriction is applied, ensuring that data management remains controlled and precise. This restriction is available for when Transport Data is disabled.   

This new feature ensures a more controlled and predictable data environment, making it easier to maintain data quality and integrity. 

Currency/Unit Conversion: 

Enhanced Flexibility and Validation for Currency and Unit Conversion Functions  

These updates bring greater flexibility to Currency Conversion Functions and introduce robust validation features for both Currency Conversion and Unit Conversion functions. These improvements are designed to ensure data accuracy, integrity, and efficiency within your financial processes.  

  1. Enhanced Flexibility in Currency Conversion Functions (Type - Banking and ERP)  

Market Data Area Configuration Now at the Assignment Level  

Previously, the Market Data Area field was configured only at the header level of the Currency Conversion function. This limitation meant that all currency conversions within a function shared the same Market Data Area, often requiring multiple Currency Conversion functions to handle different market data areas.  

With this enhancement, the Market Data Area field can now be configured at the assignment level of the Conversion Rule. This allows each conversion within a function to have its own designated Market Data Area, offering:  

  • Greater Flexibility: Tailor each currency conversion with its specific market data area, minimizing the need for multiple functions.  
  • Improved Efficiency: Manage different market data areas within a single Currency Conversion function, simplifying your setup and saving time.  

as it was

As it is now   

Dynamic Rules Adjustment 

To complement this change, the Market Data Area field has also been added to the dynamic rules screen. The logic for dynamic rules procedure generation has been adjusted to align with the new configuration options, ensuring accurate and streamlined rule generation. 

2. Comprehensive Validation for Currency and Unit Conversion Functions 

2.a. Validation for Currency Conversion Functions  

We’ve introduced extensive validation checks to ensure that all mandatory fields and dropdown selections are correctly populated in Currency Conversion functions. Key validation features include: 

  • Obligatory Fields Check: Ensures that all required fields in both the header and rules sections are filled in. If any required field is left empty, an error message stating “Value is required” will be displayed. 
  • Dropdown Selections Validation: Validates that proper items or tables are selected from dropdown menus. Incorrect selections trigger specific error messages related to the selected fields. 
  • Market Data Connection Changes: If a Market Data Connection is changed after tables have been populated, the system prompts the user to reselect appropriate tables from the newly configured Market Data Connection, ensuring consistency and accuracy. 
  • Incorrect Table Structure Detection: Validates that the selected tables match the required structure for each input field. If a table with an incorrect field structure is selected, an error message will indicate the mismatch, including details like field name, data type, and length. 
  • Random Record Entry Prevention: The system prevents random or incorrect data entries in critical fields, guiding users to provide valid data derived from the configured Market Data Connection. 
  • Rule Conversion Field Validation: Ensures that key fields in Rule Conversions, such as Source Value, Reference Date, Market Area, Conversion Rate Type, Target Value, and Target Unit, are accurately populated. Random or incorrect data entries in these fields will trigger targeted error messages. 

Example of warning message, when field is left empty in Currency Conversion Function (obligatory field check) 

2.b. Validation for Unit Conversion Functions 

We’ve also enhanced validation for Unit Conversion functions, ensuring that all mandatory fields are populated, and proper tables are selected. Key features include: 

  • Obligatory Fields Check: All required fields in both the header and rules sections must be populated, with empty fields triggering a “Value is required” error message. 
  • Dropdown Selections Validation: Validates correct table selections from dropdown menus for fields like Rates Input and Dimension Input. Incorrect selections will trigger specific error messages. 
  • Market Data Connection Changes: Prompts users to reselect appropriate tables after changing the Market Data Connection, ensuring that the correct data is used. 
  • Field Structure Validation: Checks that selected tables have the correct field structure, with error messages detailing any mismatches. 
  • Random Record Entry Prevention: Ensures that only valid data is entered into critical fields, preventing errors in Unit Conversion processes. 

Example of warning message if wrong tables are after changing the Market Data Connection in Unit Conversion Function.

Model View

Implement ordering and sequence for Model View input fields - Aggregation and Projection 

The recently introduced ordering and sequence for Model View types of Aggregation and Projections enables the users to sort the data either by sort order

and by ordering sequence: 

The sort order dictates whether the data are to be arranged either by ascending or descending order, while the ordering sequence identifies which fields are to be to be sorted first. The improvements will significantly assist users in organizing data by using multiple fields as parameters. 

Model HANA:

In a recent enhancement, Model HANAs derived from tables or virtual tables in the Database with multiple primary keys are allowed and will be registered as Key for Value help. 
Previously, using multiple keys in HANA tables within the Universal Model resulted in a validation error message stating, "HANA Tables need to have one key."  With the new enhancement, HANA tables with multiple primary keys can now be seamlessly integrated into our SAP PaPM Cloud UM.

Manage Writers:

For a modeling user, ensuring that all mandatory fields and key fields are properly assigned in Manage Writers is crucial to avoiding runtime errors. The new Fill Fields action provides a solution by listing and automatically filling in the relevant fields, offering an additional layer of security and efficiency, when creating or modifying a Writer in SAP PaPM Coud UM, whether using Model HANA, Model Entity, or connections such as OData and SAC is input/output functions.  

Key Features and Enhancements 

  1. Automated Field Assignment 

The Fill Fields action is now available at the Writer Function screen, enabling users to automatically populate all relevant key and mandatory fields in draft mode. This action ensures that essential fields from the Result Model, such as Key, Alternative Key, Mandatory fields, and fields where "Assert not null" is set to "Yes," are filled in, significantly reducing manual effort.  

  1. Real-Time Validation and Warnings 

Validation checks are conducted to ensure all necessary fields of a business object are assigned. If any mandatory fields are not assigned, or if changes occur in the key and mandatory fields of the Result Model, the user is alerted with a warning message upon saving the Writer. This proactive approach helps prevent potential errors during runtime. 

Warning Message upon pressing the button Field Fields 

Warning message if Corresponding Input Fields are not assigned  

Message upon saving the Writer when Filling Fields option is executed before initial saving 

The enhanced Fill Fields feature in the Writer Function is designed to make your modeling experience more efficient, accurate, and user-friendly.  We encourage you to explore the Fill Fields feature and integrate it into your workflow. 

Show Screen

1. Show Totals without Aggregation:

Previously, totals were tied to aggregations, but with this new enhancement, you can view them independently. Just right-click on a record, select 'Show Totals,' and see them highlighted in yellow. Need a cleaner view? Right-click again and choose ‘Hide Totals’. 

For more details, visit the SAP Help Portal --> Show Screen | SAP Help Portal. 

2.  Expanded Columns in Show Screens:

Users can now manually expand columns and save these expanded states as separate layouts in Show Screens. This functionality allows for better customization and organization of data presentation. Additionally, users can collapse some of these manually expanded groups and save the updated layout, ensuring that their preferences for viewing data are retained.

3. New "Add Grid" Option

New feature – Add Grid - has been added for users who work with Model Entities that include relations. This feature enhances the Adapt UI for Show Screens by allowing more customization and data visualization options:

When users have configured relations in their data records, the Object Page (accessible after selecting a specific data record in Show Screen) now includes an "Add Grid" option under the "+" menu in Adapt UI.

This grid can be customized, and users can create and manipulate charts within it. 

The newly added grid and charts are fully configurable.

Users can resize and move these elements within the Object Page, allowing for a highly customized data presentation that fits their specific requirements. 

Once configured, all changes, including grids and charts, are saved within the layout, ensuring that users' customizations are retained and accessible in future sessions. 

These improvements enhance user experience by providing greater control over how data is displayed and preserved across different views.

Process

Manage Processes:

1. Gantt Diagram

There are enhancements for the Gantt diagram in the Process screen. These include enabling drag functionality for activities, allowing users to resize activities to adjust their duration, and implementing both horizontal and vertical dragging with changes automatically saved to the layout. Additionally, drag-and-drop from the palette to the Gant Diagram should be possible when in edit mode.

2. Easy access to Activities from Manage Processes  

You can click on the number as a semantic link to be redirected to the Manage Activities screen, where you'll see the specific activities associated with Overdue, Active, Inactive, Confirmed, and Rejected. These semantic links are under each activity field in the General Information section of the Manage Process application. 

If you are interested in Manage processes, please feel free to look at SAP Help Portal -> Manage Processes. 

Manage Activity:

Rules Screen: Introducing the Share Button 

Want to share your Rules screen? It’s easy to access it directly from Manage Activity and sharing it is just as simple! Just click the Share button on the right side of the screen. You’ll have two options: send it via email or save it as a tile. If you choose email, a message with the screen link will pop up automatically. Opt to save it as a tile, and it’ll be pinned to your Home page for quick access anytime! 

For the full scoop on the Rules screen, don't miss our colleague's blogpost --> Dynamic Rules Screen. 

Manage Teams

Reader Team and Writer Team validation in Process, Activity, Reports and Pages 

A new enhancement was made when assigning a Reader Team and/or Writer Team in Manage Processes, Manage Activities, Manage Reports and Manage Pages. Currently, there’s an option wherein the user with the right authorizations can assign a Reader Team and/or Writer Team in a Process, Activity, Report and Page which are not created/belong to the same environment where the Process, Activity, Report and Pages were created. 

With the new enhancement, if the user assigned a Reader Team and/or Writer Team on the mentioned applications that does not belong to the same environment where the Process / Activity / Report / Pages were created, a warning message will prompt informing the user that the Reader Team and/or Writer Team does not belong to the same environment. 

For more information for Team Management, you may refer to the SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Application Help for Universal Model – Manage Teams section.

Reports

Manage Reports:

1. Enabled Renaming Fields 

Similar in Show Screen, users can now rename the displayed fields of Show Element during Edit Mode of the report by simply selecting the Edit button next to the field name. Users can also choose to apply the renaming across all Show screens within the same environment by enabling the Rename Globally in Environment checkbox. Otherwise, the renaming will only be applied to the Show Element within the report. 

2. Display Relations 

Another addition in the Columns panel of Show Elements is the Configure Relations To Display button. This allows user to add the relation configurations in the column data of the Show Element. 

3. Editing Data Records for Show Elements and Page Data Refresh 

Users can now utilize different edit mode types for data records in Show Elements of Reports. 

  • Indirect – allows user to navigate to the Object Page screen of selected data record in display mode. 
  1. Right-click Show Element, choose Edit Mode > Indirect. 
  2. Choose a specific data record to go to the Object Page screen. 
  3. Choose Edit and update the data record. 

Choose Save.

  • Direct – allows user to navigate to the Object Page screen of selected data record in directly in Edit mode (if the record is not locked by another user). 
  1. Right-click Show Element, choose Edit Mode > Direct. 
  2. Choose a specific data record to go to the Object Page screen. 
  3. Without choosing Edit, update the data record directly. 
  4. Choose Save.

  • Inline – allows user to update the data record inline the Show Element itself without navigating to the Object Page screen. 
  1. Right-click Show Element, choose Edit Mode > Inline. 
  2. Choose Edit. 
  3. Update the data record.

Choose Save (under the Edit column)

To see changes made in a specific data record without performing the actual page refresh, users can enable the Auto Refresh Page toggle found in the upper right side of the report page. Discover more exciting stuff about reports and what it can do by checking out these blogposts: Exploring Innovative Approaches to Dynamic Report Management and Enhancing Report Management with the power of generative AI copilot – Joule.

Spoiler
Possibility of editing data in Show Elements is only for applicable functions with it set of restrictions and authorizations assigned to user.

4. Expanded Columns in Show Reports:

Similar to Show Screens, users can manually expand columns and save them in Show Reports. The ability to collapse some of the expanded groups and save these adjustments ensures that the report layout reflects the user's current needs and preferences

To expand columns, go to Edit mode and then save the change

Changes are preserved upon page refreshing.

This update promotes a more efficient workflow by maintaining the state of expanded columns, allowing users to focus on data analysis and reporting without having to repeatedly adjust their views. 

Let’s bring things to a close for today.

We hope our latest updates have been exciting and helpful for you. Keep an eye out for more great features on the way in the next ‘What New in SAP PaPM Cloud Universal Model’ blogpost.

entry.title: Step-by-Step Guide to Integrate SAP BTP Cloud Foundry with IBM Instana
entry.content:
How about adding additional monitoring and performance visibility to SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) Cloud Foundry applications?
Whether you're working with SAP Ui5/Fiori, Node.js, or Java Spring Boot applications, this guide provides you with interactive steps to easily install the IBM® Instana agent and start monitoring your apps in real time.

Steps:

You need to get a SaaS license offering of IBM Instana for SAP Business Technology Platform. 

Plug & Play for SAP Ui5/Fiori Applications

  1. Insert the Instana Script:
    • Open the index.html of your SAP Ui5 application. It will be inside the webapp directory.


      • Add the following script to enable Instana monitoring: 
        <script>
  (function(s,t,a,n){s[t]||(s[t]=a,n=s[a]=function(){n.q.push(arguments)},
  n.q=[],n.v=2,n.l=1*new Date)})(window,"InstanaEumObject","ineum");
  ineum('reportingUrl', ''); // Add your reporting URL here
  ineum('key', ''); // Add your Instana key here
  ineum('trackSessions');
</script>
<script defer crossorigin="anonymous" src="https://eum.instana.io/1.6.6/eum.min.js" integrity=""></script>
  2. You're All Set:

       Deploy your application, and the Instana agent will begin monitoring immediately.

Plug & Play for Node.js Applications

  1. Install the Instana Package:

    • If using npm, run:
    npm install --save @instana/serverless-collector
    • If using yarn, run:
    yarn add @instana/serverless-collector

  2. Configure the Manifest File:

    • Update your manifest.yaml with the necessary Instana configurations. The manifest location will be at the root directory of the application:

  • applications:
- name: node-demo-api
  host: node-demo-api
  domain: cfapps.ap21.hana.ondemand.com
  buildpacks:
    - nodejs_buildpack # optional
  env:
    NODE_OPTIONS: '--require ./node_modules/@instana/serverless-collector'
    INSTANA_AGENT_KEY: '' # Add your agent key
    INSTANA_ENDPOINT_URL: '' # Add the endpoint URL
    INSTANA_LOG_LEVEL: '' # Set the log level

     

Plug & Play for Java Spring Boot Applications

  1. Download the Instana Collector:

    • Obtain the latest standalone JVM collector from the Instana repository: Instana Collector Download

  2. Update the Manifest File:

    • Add the following configurations to your manifest.yml. The manifest location will be at the root directory of the application:

 

applications:
- name: rest-demo-app
  path: ./target/rest-0.0.1-SNAPSHOT.jar
  host: rest-demo-app
  domain: cfapps.ap21.hana.ondemand.com
  env:
    JBP_CONFIG_JAVA_OPTS: 'java_opts: "-javaagent:BOOT-INF/lib/standalone-collector-jvm-[version].jar"'
    INSTANA_AGENT_KEY: '' # Add your agent key
    INSTANA_ENDPOINT_URL: '' # Add the endpoint URL
    INSTANA_LOG_LEVEL: 'TRACE' # Set the log level

 

Final Steps

Deploy Your Applications: - Push your applications to SAP BTP Cloud Foundry with the Instana agent configurations enabled. For SAP UI5/Fiori apps,i.e., multi target applications:

   mbt build

and then Deploy mta archives.

For other cloud native applications, do a

cf push

Monitor in Real-Time: - Use the Instana dashboard to monitor your applications in real-time, providing you with insights and actionable data to optimize performance and troubleshoot issues. For reference, we are leveraging the REST calls failures here:

By following these interactive steps, you can effortlessly integrate Instana with your SAP Cloud Foundry applications, providing additional monitoring and performance visibility .

Happy coding and monitoring!

entry.title: Re: Select With Internal Table
entry.content: Please paste the code in text format, because people generally don't spend time in retyping the code to test on their system.

entry.title: What is the best practice for storing attachment files that were uploaded from RAP-Fiori application
entry.content:
Hi, I start learning SAP RAP from RAP100, RAP110
and find that in the tutorial, the attachment will be kept in the created database table as RAWSTRING

Is the way it's kept the attachment as RAWSTRING in a database table best practice?

Or can we pass the attachment binary to another content server and store only the ID that can linked with the attachment in the content server?

If we can use a content server, is there any document or tutorial to show me how to do it?

Thanks,
Pakorn

entry.title: Re: Switch on gCTS (for existing packages)
entry.content:
Hi Joe,

the paradigm of the ABAP server that there can only be one active version of an object in a system at a time, remains also with gCTS - and this includes that you can only work on that one version. There is no way to allow two developers to work on different versions in the same system. The idea of the distributed development is that you have two development systems in place which are connected to the same repository. Then, each developer can work on the object in 'his' system. When he releases a transport request, he would not be able to push directly but instead would need to solve conflicts first - if the developer on the other system had pushed a newer version in the meantime which was not available in the second development system.

This option requires some special configuration for the repository once when you decide to work in a distributed environment.

You can find that described in here: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/conflict-resolution-with-gcts/ba-p/13498919

Hope this helps
Kind regards
Karin

entry.title: Re: Exciting Developments in the Auckland SAP Community: A Recap of the 2nd SAP Inside Track Event
entry.content: This is great, congrats to everyone who was involved! Much looking forward to your next event!! 😊

entry.title: How to allow an empty string with check condition in xml transformation?
entry.content:
Hi, experts.

How to allow an empty string with check condition in xml transformation?

U have this condition at mine xml transformation

<tt:cond s-check="not-initial(ref('ROOT.PROVIDER.BRANCHCODE'))">
  <branchCode tt:value-ref="ROOT.PROVIDER.BRANCHCODE"/>
</tt:cond>

and call the transformation

call transformation (LV_TRANS_NAME)
            source xml XML_FINAL2
            result ROOT = IT_TAB.

If document has empty <branchCode></branchCode> - everything is ok, but some documents have

space inside ( <branchCode></branchCode> )and i'm getting dump. 

How to allow an space and save check to not initial.
Type of BRANCHCODE - CHAR4.

entry.title: Re: Unable to save nested handling units
entry.content:
Hi 

I was checking your screen shot so I see something different in status why it is not green. It shows a warning. Could you check what is that  I have highlighted in yellow 

 

Regards

Rashid Khan

entry.title: Re: Switch on gCTS (for existing packages)
entry.content:
Hi Sunil,

sorry, I have missed your question before...

the steps back are somehow the revers-order:

  • stop all development on the object
  • release all open transport requests where the object is part of
  • Make sure that the latest commit is aktive in your system
  • Change the transport layer of the object

The versions of the object that you created while using gCTS will remain part of the repository (if you don't delete it). And in the development system, you do also have for sure all transport requests that where created when you developed using gCTS. In target systems, you might not have all versions of the object that had been created while using gCTS if not every commit was imported. The transport requests in the target system do have a different naming - so you cannot find the imports of the respective object under the ID of the development system.

Hope this helps
Kind regards
Karin

entry.title: Re: CDS VIEW ENTITY - CURR / QUAN calculated field gives...
entry.content:
HI,

Could you please try with the below.

@Semantics.quantity.unitOfMeasure: 'rfccr'

CURR_TO_DECFLOAT_AMOUNT( bseg.dmbtr  ) * GET_NUMERIC_VALUE( menge ) as dmbtr_per_menge.

Regards,

Pradeep.

entry.title: Re: Live Data Connection To Manufacturing Data Objects for Analytics
entry.content:
Hi Harry,

Great detailed explanation of how to create the DataSphere to DM connection. We're trying to follow this to establish our own connection but can't seem to replicate the HANA Live Connection steps as we only see SAP BW and SAP S/4HANA as options under Connect to Live Data.

Are we perhaps missing another configuration step to enable a live connection to HANA?

 

Thanks,

Cal

entry.title: Re: Standard SAP OData service. Get formatted document texts
entry.content: In that case I think you would want to submit a customer influence item, otherwise you look at extension/custom development.

entry.title: Re: Troubleshooting SAP Certification discount code for special programs
entry.content: Hi @Omarnabhan85 - This is not an activation code, but a campaign code. Please refer to the redemption guide in the email where you got the code from.

entry.title: Re: Troubleshooting SAP Certification discount code for special programs
entry.content: @okerekenicholas -  If you find yourself with the code successfully applied, but at a "dead-end" without a check-out button, your request has more than likely been submitted. Please allow a couple of days for processing and you should then get your confirmation via email. Sorry about that!

entry.title: Re: Troubleshooting SAP Certification discount code for special programs
entry.content: @S2285PIH - it looks like you are purchasing the wrong product!

entry.title: Re: Plant for FSD type 001 does not belongs to company code
entry.content:
Hello Parvez,

Please check the PPOME and check the company code assigned to your purchase organization.

The error is coming because the company code and plants used for FSD are different.

Best Regards,

Adwait

entry.title: Missing Business Partner View, Fields, Purchasing Data
entry.content:
Hello together,

after the Customer-Vendor-Integration (CVI) in ECC we have missing fields and views in the Business Partner.

We checked the fieldmodification in customizing and find nothing. Do you have a recommendation for us?

We missing the deletion flag, deletion blog in the view "Vendor General Data" and in the "purchasing data the register cards...

Many thanks in advance.

BR

Manuel

entry.title: Re: Planned order not consuming inventory
entry.content: Hi Caetano, please see attached image. We have stock 5 Qty, yet planned order generated. Please advise. Thanks.

entry.title: Re: S/4 HANA Product compliance - Safety Data Sheet creat...
entry.content:
Hi Christoph and Saskia.

Thank you both for your detailed responses. 

To be clear, we will only be implementing on-premise 2023. Cloud will not be an option. 

To explore Saskia's recommendation for alternatives further, can you check the below to make sure I understand what you said correctly?

1. Product data is maintained in product compliance module on compliance view / "CCI" - phrases, composition etc 

2. Use external application such as WWI for SDS design and connect the product compliance module to it via API/BADI. Data gets sent to that application to generate the SDS

3. Upload the SDS in the product compliance module

Thanks

entry.title: Webinar on regulatory changes in Spain (Spanish HCM User Group AUSAPE)
entry.content:
Join us at the Webinar Grupo de Trabajo RRHH (AUSAPE) to receive a detailed explanation from the SAP expert about all recent and upcoming regulatory changes in Spain.
Some other latest enhancements will be discussed: updated Contract Forms and cases of relapse absences in FIE when there is integration between Employee Central and SAP Payroll or Employee Central Payroll.

Speaker: Gema Moraleda, Localization Product Manager, SAP Globalization, SAP

This event is targeted at members of AUSAPE. The webinar will be conducted in Spanish. 

Registration link

Best Regards ,

entry.title: One or more rows contain values violating non-null, unique, or foreign-key constraints.
entry.content:
We execute SELECT statements in our .NET 8.0 app via the ODBC driver on HANA systems and receive the error message mentioned in the subject line.

Characteristics of the affected system:
- SAP Business One 10.0 version for SAP HANA (10.00.260) SP 2408 (64-bit)
- HDBODBC (64-bit) 2.20.22.39682

The problem only occurs when SAP fields are retrieved that have the data type NVARCHAR(1) with a default value. For example: OWHS.Locked
SELECT "Locked" FROM "OWHS"

The problem no longer occurs for these fields if their data type is changed via the CAST command, e.g. to VARCHAR(1).
SELECT CAST("Locked" AS VARCHAR(1)) FROM "OWHS"

In all other systems that are equipped with older SAP Businnes One and HDBODBC versions, this problem does not occur.

Our workaround:
Disable the observance of constraints, .NET-Code:
DataSet dataSet = new DataSet { EnforceConstraints = false };
DataTable result = dataSet.Tables.Add();
ExecuteOnDbCommand(command, commandAction => result.Load(commandAction.ExecuteReader()), setSchemaContext);
return result;

entry.title: Re: Employment end date notification - old alert
entry.content:
Hi @altugatas

Thank you for your response. The old alert is deleted when the effective start date of the new record is prior to the effective start date of the older alert. For example, if we have a contract end date of December 13, 2024, the alert would be triggered 90 days before on September 14 and sent out.

However, if we add a new record with a date of December 14, 2024, indicating a contract renewal, the original alert that would have been sent out on September 14 is still sent out, even if we have a new record on December 14, 2024. This happens despite having the "Set Alert.Delete Old Pending Alert Flag" rule set to Yes.

If we create a new record with an effective date of September 12, 2024, which is two days prior to the September 14 trigger date. By doing this, the alert will not be sent out, and a new alert will be triggered based on the new end date, which is 90 days before the contract renewal date.

However, please note that this approach will result in a record on September 12, 2024, with a new contract end date, while the effective contract renewal should be on December 14, 2024. therefore it seems alert are working with the effective date of the record. The transition from a limited contract to an unlimited one should only occur once the limited contract has ended. At the current date, there should be no record of the transition from limited to unlimited.

Thank you in advance 

Giorgio

entry.title: Re: What Are You Looking Forward to Learning or Doing at Devtoberfest This Year?
entry.content: My top reason for attending Devtoberfest is to stay updated with the latest trends and technologies in ABAP development. It’s a fantastic opportunity to learn, grow, and contribute to the community. Plus, it’s my first time attending Devtoberfest, and I’m eager to make the most of this experience!

entry.title: Re: Live Data Connection To Manufacturing Data Objects for Analytics
entry.content:
Hi Cal,

I realized the same, there seems to be a change in the latest version of Datasphere. You can now skip the first step and only assure the certificates are uploaded as described. It should then be sufficient to create the connection for the space you are in. This is described in the section further below.

I tested in my current environment and it works. I will try to update the blog accordingly.

entry.title: What is TAANA in SAP?
entry.content:
TAANA Transaction vs. SE16N: A Comparative Analysis

Understanding TAANA and SE16N

TAANA and SE16N are two commonly used transactions in SAP for data maintenance and analysis. While they share the same underlying purpose of providing a platform for data access and manipulation, they cater to different use cases and offer distinct functionalities.

TAANA: A Transaction for Table Maintenance

  • Purpose: Primarily designed for maintaining data in custom or standard tables.
  • Functionality: Offers a user-friendly interface with fields and buttons for creating, editing, and deleting table entries.
  • Features:
    • Field help and description for better understanding of data elements.
    • Checkboxes for mandatory fields and validation rules.
    • Option to display table entries in a list view.

SE16N: A General Data Browser

  • Purpose: A versatile tool for browsing and analyzing data from various SAP objects, including tables, views, and data clusters.
  • Functionality: Provides a more flexible approach to data exploration, allowing users to define selection criteria and perform data manipulations.
  • Features:
    • Advanced search options and filters.
    • Ability to export data to various formats (e.g., Excel, CSV).
    • Integration with other SAP tools like ABAP debugger.

Key Differences

Feature TAANA SE16N
Primary PurposeTable maintenanceData browsing and analysis
InterfaceUser-friendly, field-basedMore flexible, selection-based
FunctionalityCreate, edit, delete table entriesExplore data from various objects
FeaturesMandatory fields, validationAdvanced search, data export
Export to Sheets

When to Use Which

  • TAANA: Use TAANA when you need to directly modify data in a specific table, especially for routine maintenance tasks.
  • SE16N: Choose SE16N when you want to explore data from multiple sources, analyze trends, or perform more complex data manipulations.

Running TAANA in SAP: A Step-by-Step Guide

TAANA is a transaction code in SAP used for maintaining data in custom or standard tables. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to run TAANA:

1. Access the SAP System

  • Log into your SAP system using your credentials.

2. Enter TAANA

  • In the SAP Easy Access menu, navigate to the Transaction field.
  • Enter TAANA and press Enter.

3. Select the Table

  • In the Table Name field, enter the name of the table you want to maintain. For example, if you want to maintain data in the MARA table (material master), enter MARA.
  • Press Enter.

4. Choose Maintenance Mode

  • You'll be presented with options for maintenance mode:
    • Display: View table entries without editing.
    • Change: Edit existing table entries.
    • Create: Add new table entries.
    • Delete: Remove existing table entries.
  • Select the appropriate mode based on your requirements.

5. Enter Selection Criteria

  • If you want to filter the table entries based on specific criteria, enter the values in the corresponding fields. For instance, to find materials with a specific material group, enter the group in the MATNR field.

6. Execute the Query

  • Click the Execute button to retrieve the table entries based on your selection criteria.

7. Perform Maintenance

  • Depending on the maintenance mode you selected, you can:
    • Display: View the table entries.
    • Change: Edit existing fields by double-clicking on them and entering new values.
    • Create: Add new table entries by entering values in the required fields and clicking Save.
    • Delete: Select the entries you want to delete and click Delete.

8. Save Changes

  • After making your changes, click the Save button to store them in the database.

9. Exit TAANA

  • To exit the transaction, click the Exit button or press Ctrl+F3.

Remember:

  • Always exercise caution when modifying data in SAP. Incorrect changes can have significant consequences.
  • If you're unsure about the implications of your actions, consult with a SAP expert or refer to the system's documentation.

By following these steps, you can effectively use TAANA to maintain data in SAP tables.

Conclusion

Both TAANA and SE16N are essential tools for SAP users. Understanding their differences and knowing when to use each can significantly improve your efficiency and effectiveness in data management and analysis.

entry.title: Re: Issue with concatenate. No blanks at the end. RESPECTING BLANKS not working fully.
entry.content:
Hi @Sandra_Rossi,
I did declare ls_tab like this- DATA: ls_tab TYPE string. 
I also tried it like this ls_tab TYPE c LENGTH 60. And like ls_tab(60).
Non of it made any difference. 

DATA: BEGIN OF ls_low,
        ed_broj TYPE zed_broj-ed_broj,
      END OF ls_low,
      ls_high       LIKE ls_low,
      lt            LIKE TABLE OF ls_low,
      so_ed         TYPE zrtv_ed_broj_range_t,
      ls_ed         TYPE zrtv_ed_broj_range,
      functioncall1 TYPE i,
      brojac1       TYPE i.

DATA : brojac    TYPE n LENGTH 8,
       high      TYPE n LENGTH 8,
       task1(30).

DATA: lt_ed_broj    TYPE TABLE OF zed_broj,
      ls_ed_broj    TYPE          zed_broj,
      lt_ed_broj_pl TYPE TABLE OF zed_broj,
      lt_ed_broj_fl TYPE TABLE OF zed_broj.

DATA: lt_ed_broj_r TYPE TABLE OF zed_broj_r,
      ls_ed_broj_r TYPE zed_broj_r.

DATA: lt_pogoni     TYPE TABLE OF zrtv_pogoni,
      ls_pogoni     TYPE zrtv_pogoni,
      lt_snabdevaci TYPE TABLE OF zrtv_snabdevaci,
      ls_snabdevaci TYPE zrtv_snabdevaci,
      lt_oznaka_ods TYPE TABLE OF zrtv_oznaka_ods,
      ls_oznaka_ods TYPE zrtv_oznaka_ods,
      lv_tabix      TYPE sytabix.

PARAMETERS: pa_per TYPE n LENGTH 7 DEFAULT 100000.

PARAMETERS: amount TYPE bp_betrw_kk DEFAULT 150,
            p_date TYPE sydatum     DEFAULT sy-datum.

PARAMETERS: p_file_o TYPE string.


PERFORM kreiraj_datoteku_zad USING lt_ed_broj_fl ''.



FORM kreiraj_datoteku_zad  USING    pt_ed_broj TYPE ty_zed_broj_t
                                    VALUE(p_pravno_ind).

  DATA: lv_filename  TYPE string,
        ls_tab type c LENGTH 60, "ls_tab(60),
*        ls_tab TYPE STRING,
        lt_tab       LIKE TABLE OF ls_tab,
        lv_row_cnt   TYPE i,
        lv_snabdevac TYPE zrtv_snabdevac,
        ls_ed_broj   TYPE zed_broj,
        lv_empty     type c,
        mmyy(4).


  CONCATENATE p_date+4(2) p_date+2(2) INTO mmyy.

   APPEND VALUE #( oznaka_ods_region = 'NS  '
                  pogon            = '    '
                  ed_broj          = '5005965911               '
                  SIFRA_RAZMENE    = '106000071120             '
                  snabdevac        = '12345' ) TO pt_ed_broj.

    APPEND VALUE #( oznaka_ods_region = 'NS  '
                  pogon            = '    '
                  ed_broj          = '5005965922               '
                  SIFRA_RAZMENE    = '106000072220             '
                  snabdevac        = '12345' ) TO pt_ed_broj.

    APPEND VALUE #( oznaka_ods_region = 'NS  '
                  pogon            = '    '
                  ed_broj          = '5005965933               '
                  SIFRA_RAZMENE    = '106000073320             '
                  snabdevac        = '12345' ) TO pt_ed_broj.

    APPEND VALUE #( oznaka_ods_region = 'NS  '
                  pogon            = '    '
                  ed_broj          = '5005965944               '
                  SIFRA_RAZMENE    = '106000075520             '
                  snabdevac        = '12345' ) TO pt_ed_broj.


  SORT pt_ed_broj BY snabdevac.
  DESCRIBE TABLE pt_ed_broj LINES lv_row_cnt.
  LOOP AT pt_ed_broj INTO ls_ed_broj.
    lv_tabix = sy-tabix.
    IF lv_snabdevac <> ls_ed_broj-snabdevac AND
       lv_tabix GT 1.
      OPEN DATASET lv_filename FOR OUTPUT IN TEXT MODE ENCODING DEFAULT.
      LOOP AT lt_tab INTO ls_tab.
        TRANSFER ls_tab TO lv_filename.
      ENDLOOP.
      CLOSE DATASET lv_filename.
      REFRESH lt_tab.
      IF p_pravno_ind EQ 'X'.
        CONCATENATE p_file_o 'TEST_' ls_ed_broj-snabdevac '_PL_ZADUZENI_' mmyy '.txt'
        INTO lv_filename.
      ELSE.
        CONCATENATE p_file_o 'TEST_' ls_ed_broj-snabdevac '_FL_ZADUZENI_' mmyy '.txt'
        INTO lv_filename.
      ENDIF.

    ELSEIF  lv_tabix = 1.
      IF p_pravno_ind EQ 'X'.
        CONCATENATE p_file_o 'TEST_' ls_ed_broj-snabdevac '_PL_ZADUZENI_' mmyy '.txt'
        INTO lv_filename.
      ELSE.
        CONCATENATE p_file_o 'TEST_' ls_ed_broj-snabdevac '_FL_ZADUZENI_' mmyy '.txt'
        INTO lv_filename.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.

*    lv_empty = '.'.
    CONCATENATE ls_ed_broj-oznaka_ods_region(4)
                ls_ed_broj-pogon(4)
                ls_ed_broj-ed_broj(25) space space
               ls_ed_broj-SIFRA_RAZMENE(25)  INTO ls_tab RESPECTING BLANKS.
    APPEND ls_tab TO lt_tab.
    lv_snabdevac  = ls_ed_broj-snabdevac.
  ENDLOOP.
  IF lt_tab[] IS NOT INITIAL.


   p_file_o = 'C:\testinv\' && lv_filename.

* TESTING GUI DOWNLOAD
CALL FUNCTION 'GUI_DOWNLOAD'
  EXPORTING

    FILENAME                        = p_file_o
    TRUNC_TRAILING_BLANKS_EOL = abap_false
  TABLES
    DATA_TAB                        = lt_tab
    .
IF SY-SUBRC <> 0.
* Implement suitable error handling here
ENDIF.
ENDIF.

*TESTING TRANSFER
IF lt_tab[] IS NOT INITIAL.
    OPEN DATASET lv_filename FOR OUTPUT IN TEXT MODE ENCODING DEFAULT.
    LOOP AT lt_tab INTO ls_tab.
      TRANSFER ls_tab TO lv_filename.
    ENDLOOP.
    CLOSE DATASET lv_filename.
    REFRESH lt_tab.
  ENDIF.
ENDFORM.

Here is my complete testing program. GUI_DOWNLOAD is now working properly, but TRANSFER part is not. It creates .txt document that I can see in AL11:

But when I try to save it on local PC, there are no blanks at the end of rows. 
Thanks again for the effort. If you have any new suggestions, I would really appreciate it. 

BR.

entry.title: Re: SAP Adobe Forms - Question mark field
entry.content: You could check OSS note 2431785 to see if it's relevant.

entry.title: Re: Issue with Dynamic Table Access for Copying MOLGA Content Between Tables
entry.content:
Hi,

The field symbol to which the data is selected is not assigned to any internal table.

" Select data from source country
  SELECT * FROM (pv_tabname) INTO TABLE <lt_src_data> WHERE country = pv_src_ctry.

  1. An instance of an internal table has to be created dynamically for the name of the DB table the selection will be done for.
  2. Field symbol should be assigned to the instance of the internal table.
  3. Selection can be done from the DB into the field symbol (therefore into the instance of the internal table).

Kind regards,
Mateusz

The problem is, the BADI executes only once, and I am able to fetch only one record at a time and show it in the adobe form, what I need is to fetch multiple records at once and display them all in the adobe form template

Regards,

Nikunj Mehta

Version 1.2.1 was released, which is supposed to fix the error.

But unfortunately, we are now seeing an other error:
"Couldn't import object 'SAP_GEN_HL_DateGridText [SAP_CONTENT\SAP_GEN_HL_DateGridText]' : statusNotAcceptedForRepoHttpClient: Status 500 is not accepted: tempItem.indexOf is not a function"

Does 1.2.1 work for you?

entry.title: Re: How to allow an empty string with check condition in xml transformation?
entry.content:
s-check applies only to Serialization. You are currently Deserializing (SOURCE XML).

NB: don't confuse space (one space) and empty string (zero space).

entry.title: Compare a Date value with Today's date and output a text value in success factors
entry.content:
Hi all,

Probably a very simple question (I've literally had 4 hours experience in success factors reporting - don't ask)

I need to create a calculated column to compare a Date value with Today's date and output text values.
If Date < Today Then "Past Date"  Else "Current or Future Date"

Really basic stuff yet I can't suss it out  😞

I have to say even BOBJ Reporter back in 2000 was a million times better than SF reporting - it really is dire.

entry.title: Re: Unable to connect new CF accounts to cloud connector
entry.content: Hi was this resolved? Facing the same issue

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello Dear,

My career commenced in 2017 with a one-month internship, following which I graduated in 2018.

In 2019, I pursued a course in SAP Production Planning (PP) and subsequently took on various roles within the ERP domain. In 2020, I was offered a position as an ERP System Specialist with a Company, where I initially worked with Microsoft Dynamics AX. After a year, the company undertook an upgrade to SAP S/4HANA, marking the beginning of my journey with SAP.

In my initial role as an Application Support Specialist, I gained valuable experience with the logistics cycle, including the MM, PP, and QM modules, and resolved numerous issues. This experience significantly deepened my expertise in the PP and QM modules.

After 1.5 years, I transitioned to a new role as a PP and QM Consultant with Another Company , where I expanded my knowledge further and participated in a comprehensive End-to-End (E2E) implementation.

To further enhance my skills and credentials, I am seeking certification in SAP Quality Management (QM) and would greatly appreciate your support in this endeavor.

entry.title: Re: 2024 Early Fall Stammtisch @ Legacy Hall
Re: 2024 Early Fall Stammtisch @ Legacy Hall
entry.content: thank you to all who attended and shared their stories at the Legacy Hall, early fall stammtisch. Topics discussed: Fiori apps, BTP services, Event Mesh, IBP, CAP & RAP...   our thinking heads started talking about 2025 events already and plan to have a code jam, an inside track early next year after the SAP events this fall.

entry.title: Importing Scope from the Digital Discovery Assessment into SAP Central Business Configuration
entry.content:
Are you leveraging the digital discovery assessment toolkit for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition? If so, you'll be pleased to know that you can seamlessly import your scope selection from there into SAP Central Business Configuration with just a few clicks. This integration between the two tools has been available for several releases now, providing greater convenience and efficiency for users.

Screenshot of the Select Country/Region dialog featuring the Import Scope from JSON File button

Once you’ve completed your scope selection in the digital discovery assessment, the next step is to export it as a JSON file. This file can then be easily imported into SAP Central Business Configuration when defining your scope. The JSON file must be maintained in a specific format, which is automatically generated when exporting from the digital discovery assessment. For use cases going beyond that, a sample JSON file can be downloaded from SAP Central Business Configuration. This file can be manually adjusted as needed and be used as a template.

Screenshot of the Import Scope from JSON File dialog including the button to download a sample file

Upon import, a dialog will notify you of the success of the import and whether any imported scope requires your attention. It's important to note that the import process does not impact any previous scope selections made within SAP Central Business Configuration.

Screenshot of the Warning dialog pointing to imported scenarios that need special attention

To learn more, you can go to this SAP Help Portal page for step-by-step instructions and further details about this topic. And to stay in the know, make sure to follow the SAP Central Business Configuration tag. Or follow this dedicated blog collection to stay up to date with the latest blog posts.

Do you have an idea on how to further enhance SAP Central Business Configuration? Go to the SAP Customer Influence portal to submit an improvement request or vote for the ideas of others. This helps us understand your requirements even better and prioritize our work accordingly. We’re looking forward to your improvement requests.

entry.title: Using Fiori 8D problem solving for defect created in S/4 Hana using SAP QM
entry.content:
Hello
I've just completed a migration from ECC to S/4 Hana. My QM manager need to manage a 8D report on Defects created in S/4 Hana with QM01 tarnsaction or similar. I know which Porblem solving 8M metodology is available only in FIORI using APP F2929. I can manage 8D procedure only for defects created directly in FIORI using Create defect Tile, but I'm not able to Process defects previously created on S/4 QM environment (QM01 or similar). Someone can suggest to me a solution ? Is it possible manage  8D Metodolgy in Fiori for defects created in S/4 QM too ?
Thank-you in advance for your help
Claudio

entry.title: GTS tables related to currencies fr instance TCURF
entry.content:
I noticed that there are differences in the TCURF table between GTS and ECC GTS tables related to currencies for instance  TCURF,

Question I have is the following: Is there a need to bring the currencies related tables up to date with identical entries in the ECC and GTS systems?

entry.title: Re: Padding is invalid and cannot be removed
entry.content: And did you impliment the suggested work arounds on that post?

entry.title: Re: Required Learning on New Learning Home Experience
entry.content:
Hi Wayne,

Regarding the change of a label content, it is (almost) always possible - read here for the general explanation: https://me.sap.com/notes/2120336/E.

Bear in mind though that the same label may appear on different screens - possibly in different contexts.

Changing the value of the label should be exectuded with caution and always keep a backup of the original label value, should you need to revert to it.

For "Required Learning", search label id label.RequiredAssignments

KR, Frederic

entry.title: Re: One item and classes with different durations?
entry.content:
Hi @Guido,

The item duration can be adjusted at the subsequent class level by adjusting the class segments themselves.

I suggest you download our SAP SuccessFactors HCM Training Materials Guide from the Digital Library at https://dam.sap.com/mac/app/p/pdf/asset/preview/MqoPXdx?ltr=a&rc=10

And use p179 of Admin Help at https://help.sap.com/doc/844cba6db4b0477e92867df6c24a2109/2405/en-US/SF_Admin_Help_en.pdf

If a class presents a different duration that the "default" duration at the item level, it will reflect in the library view when displaying the various available classes.

Hope that helps

KR, Frederic

entry.title: Re: Z1 ledger and accounting principle related issue whil...
entry.content:
Hello, The ledgers to be posted by CKMLCP are defined by ledgere group ''Ledger Group Settlement (FI)'' 'in V_FCML_MLCO_COFI.

For the depreciation run, you have to define accounting principles that are in agreement with your asset accounting configuration. In this case you define accouting principle ZMLP in the COGM configuration but this accouting principle is not used in asset accouting, causing conflicts when executing AFAB.

entry.title: Re: SAP Slipstream Engine: Keyboard opens automatically i...
entry.content: It depends on transaction. You create onload script, identify the object like in transaction IH08 and put this in script session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/box%B010002_BLOCK_1000").setFocus(); (then you attach this script in OnLoad event)

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Integration for data services - Product Positioning and Direction
entry.content:
From note 3494037: 

  • September 2024
    • SAP launches the Tenant Migration Guide on the SAP Help Portal
    •  
    • Where is the guide?

entry.title: Re: SAC - Blending Support in Optimized Design Mode, BW l...
entry.content:
We support Blended Charts with the available release in the Optimized Experience. For Blended Tables it is tentatively planned for the 2025 Q1 QRC (subject to change by SAP at any point in time). It is best to continue to monitor the Roadmap Explorer to see the latest updates and exact timelines.

For Blended Visualizations, we've added a more guided experience to ensure that customers are not creating a Blended Visualization without realizing OR assume there are in a blended state. It means there are two rules:

  1. Users must use at least one entity from each data model that is added to the Builder Panel. It could be a measure or dimension and can be satisfied via binding it to the visualization, adding a filter, and so on
  2. Users must have a least one linked dimension within the visualization.

entry.title: Re: Access Fiori apps via SAP Build Work Zone Site with Federated SAP S/4HANA Content

entry.title: Re: Directly call create object page or RAP action from Tile
entry.content: Thanks Mio for your reply. But that means I have to create 2 apps, 1 for general display and other for create.

entry.title: Where can I learn to to streamline and automate bank statement processing?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Discovering Bank Statement Processing and Account Clearing in SAP S/4HANA learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey empowers business users and consultants to streamline and automate bank statement processing. In doing so, they will be able to boost efficiency in the finance sector and effectively utilize the capabilities of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Learning Objectives

  • Describe cash collection and reconciliation​
  • Define how cash is applied in the system​
  • Describe the Multi-Bank Connectivity option
  • Monitor the status of bank statements
  • Describe configured posting rules​
  • Describe automated posting rules​
  • Reprocess bank statements​
  • Receivables line-item matching​
  • Payables line-item matching​

 

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge.

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Public Cloud - CDS Development - Eclipse
entry.content: Prasanth, Thank you for the link. It has so much information and the simple answers I am looking for may be somehwre in the document. I have built the CDS and using Service Binding as OData V2 - API I am able to see the data. But I am looking to find ways whether there are any simple options and get to what I am looking for. Any specific answer to those pointed questions woul dhelp very much.

entry.title: Re: Query report of Bin location and Batch SAP Business One
entry.content:
Try the below query, Replace or Remove the item code as required.

Select x."ItemCode", x."BatchID", x."BatchNumber", x."ExpDate", x."BinLocation", x."Qty"
From(
Select a."ItemCode", a."SnBMDAbs" as "BatchID", c."DistNumber" as "BatchNumber", c."ExpDate", b."BinCode" as "BinLocation",
Sum(a."OnHandQty") as "Qty"
From OBBQ a
Inner Join OBIN b on b."AbsEntry" = a."BinAbs"
Inner Join OBTN c on c."AbsEntry" = a."SnBMDAbs" AND a."ItemCode" = c."ItemCode"
Where a."ItemCode" = '10001'
Group By a."ItemCode", a."SnBMDAbs", b."BinCode", c."DistNumber", c."ExpDate"
) as X
Where x."Qty" != 0

entry.title: Re: SAP Adobe Forms - Question mark field
entry.content: Thanks for the reply. I already checed this note, i have English translations in the system for those Balance confirmation PDF forms. I cant't understand why those fields are not editable. Maybe the fastest solution is to delete those tables and recreate them from scrach.

entry.title: Re: SAP Slipstream Engine: Keyboard opens automatically i...
entry.content:
Hi,

If the problem is with MENU text field, which has initial focus, then there are several options to try.

  1. Hide or make Menu text field read-only (if you are not using it)
  2. Use script for onAfterRefresh, which will move Focus from text field to its label, for example:
// onAfterRefresh move focus from Menu input to Label
if (session.findById("wnd[0]").systemFocus.id == "wnd[0]/usr/subX:/SCWM/SAPLRF_SSCR_PM:0001/txt/SCWM/S_RF_SCRELM-MENU") {
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/subX:/SCWM/SAPLRF_SSCR_PM:0001/lbl/SCWM/S_RF_SCRELM-MENU").setFocus();
}
You have to check the IDs of Label and Menu text field (especially SAPLRF_SSCR_PM:0001 part), because they can differ on your screens.
You can add some other fields into this logic too.
 
I hope this will work.

Best Regards!

entry.title: Re: Mac/Linux trigger Java GUI Scripting from command line
entry.content:
In case someone comes across this thread and that they're having issues with SAPGUI for Java 7.80:

  • The NOPHANTOM trace appears to be replaced with SCRIPTNOMESSAGE
    • The reason why this works is because setting this trace (as a side effect) puts SAPGUI into "unattended mode"
  • If you rather not use SCRIPTNOMESSAGE (and see unnecessary tracing) then at the beginning of your script you can do the following (undocumented) trick:
    • application.unwrap(application).mUnattended = true;
  • I know there are references in this thread about how the SAPGUI can/will exit after the script completes and elsewhere in the SAPGUI documentation it suggests that closing connections/sessions should shutdown the SAPGUI -- but this has not been my experience. 
    • After closing the connections/sessions and confirming it completed with the SHUTDOWN trace, at the end of my script I now call:
      • java.lang.System.exit(0);
    • NOTE: This will not work with the Java Security policy that is shipped with the SAPGUI.  I had to call the SAPGUI using Java (like the author originally did) and pass in the absolute path for a custom security policy to the JVM at start up:
      • java -Djava.security.policy=file://...
    • The contents of the security policy I used are:
      • grant principal com.sap.platin.micro.GuiPrincipal "SAPGUI:UserScript" {
    permission java.io.FilePermission "<<ALL FILES>>", "read,write,delete,execute";
    permission java.lang.RuntimePermission "getenv.*";
    // Generic Permissions necessary for Scripting
    permission javax.security.auth.AuthPermission "doAsPrivileged";
    permission java.awt.AWTPermission "createRobot";
 
    // Allow the script to exit
    permission java.lang.RuntimePermission "exitVM";
};

entry.title: Re: SAP Mobile Service Client shows several blank screens.
entry.content:
When dynamic pages are not rendering most likely the problem is invalid metadata.  I would suggest logging the generated metadata and paste into a new blank temp page in the Editor and look for errors.

For your caption issue can you provide more details around that call.  Maybe share the relevant code leading up to that line.

entry.title: Re: SAP Adobe Forms - Question mark field
entry.content: Yes, deleting the fields and recreating them is the quickest alternative, and I tested it out in our dev system because we have the same display on our version (S4 2020).

entry.title: Re: sap BTP MDK deployee the changes every time i am gett...
entry.content: I would recommend checking the logs in Mobile Services as well as checking the logs in the backend.  Hopefully there will be more details as to what exactly failed that will help you troubleshoot the backend error.

entry.title: Re: AUC Automatic Creation from WBS - S/4 HANA
entry.content: this is the perfect document i was looking for, i will execute the steps and let you know about my test cases and observations if any. Thank you so very much for your kind gesture of sharing the knowledge with all.

entry.title: Re: Planned order not consuming inventory
entry.content:
Hello

You are using a make-to-order planning strategy/requirement type, therefore, system won't consider existing stock and it will always generate a new planned order.

I suggest you to use a make-to-stock planning strategy (like 40) and then try with a new CIR.

Regards,
Caetano

entry.title: Re: Venda de Serviço com ISS retido - erro ao salvar a ordem de venda - condição BX63
entry.content: Link has been updated

entry.title: Re: sap BTP MDK deployee the changes every time i am gett...
entry.content: Will you plz share the images it would be better to understand

entry.title: Re: Estorno - ERRO Qtd.EF
entry.content:
Hello.

Here we are dealing with this error in an order that had an invoice linked to it issued in the previous month, and since the period was already closed, this generated the error. The quantity in free use did not exist because the control lot had been reversed, but since the invoice was not reversed, the system prevented the processing from continuing with this error message. Please check if there is an invoice in a previous period and if it is possible to reverse it before continuing with this reversal process using movement 102.

Adilson

 

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Olá. 

Aqui tratamos esse erro num pedido que tinha a nota fiscal atrelada a ele lançada no mes anterior, e , como o periodo já estava fechado, isso gerava o erro. A quantidade em livre utilização não existia pois, o lote de controle fora estornado, mas, como não estornaram a nota fiscal, o sistema impedia com essa mensagem de erro que o processamento seguisse. Por favor, verifique se existe nota fiscal em periodo anterior e se é possivel estorna-la antes de seguir com esse processo de estorno usando o movimento 102.

Adilson

entry.title: Re: sap BTP MDK deployee the changes every time i am gett...
entry.content:
Hi @tirumala 

There are 2 ways to troubleshoot and find the root cause.

1. Firstly, You can check the logs in the mobile services cockpit, reference screenshot below. This will give some idea as to why the initialization is failing.

2. In the backend, you can check IWFND/ERROR_LOG or IWBEP/ERROR_LOG.

 

Thank you.

entry.title: SAC - Scale percentage in the model
entry.content:
Hi All,

in my analytic model can I set the scale "percentage" for a measure?

For example I have this calculation in the model , but I don't find the way to set as percentageeee:

the only way I've found is:

1. Create a formula in the story that links to this measure

2. Formatting this formula in the settings

I'd like to avoid to create a formula, but I don't understand if I nee to do something in the model or in the story.

 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Guided Framework for Simplified Organizational Structure Set up in CBC for SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content: Can we Download Organizational Structure from CBC ?
